title: Avenula bromoides
taxonomy: Avenula bromoides is classified in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is placed in the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae. Within the tribe Poeae, it is further categorized in the subtribe Poinae. The genus Avenula is closely related to the genus Koeleria and both are sometimes placed together in the genus Koeleria. Avenula bromoides is a species of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe.
uses: Avenula bromoides, also known as meadow oat-grass, is a lesser-known species with potential medicinal properties. It contains saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's roots and leaves may have uses in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In some cultures, Avenula bromoides is used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential.

side_effects: Avenula bromoides, commonly known as false oat-grass, does not have well-documented side effects as it is typically not used for medicinal purposes. Ingesting large quantities of the plant may also lead to gastrointestinal issues. It is also possible that Avenula bromoides may interact with medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Avenula bromoides.
